publicSideBand: Providence Water replaces the public side at no cost when the owner participates
privateSideBand: Private side can be replaced for free in funded contracts or financed through a 10-year 0 percent loan
fullReplacementBand: Full replacement can be free in funded contracts and otherwise uses the loan pathway
housingTypeAssumption: Providence Water published program options rather than a fixed bid range
permitAssumption: Program timing depends on whether the address is in a planned contract
restorationAssumption: Restoration and final scope depend on the active contract or private-side loan project
methodologySummary: Providence Water frames cost through two utility-specific paths rather than a single price: free replacement inside planned federally and state-funded contracts and a separate 10-year 0 percent loan path outside that queue.
ownerTriggerSummary: Owners avoid direct cost only when the property is already inside Providence Water's funded contract schedule and otherwise must use the separate loan workflow to replace the private side before the utility replaces its portion.
